text: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander reportedly wins second straight NBA MVP award - Yahoo Sports
fullText: The Thunder star guard is the 14th player in NBA history to win back-to-back MVPs

text: Alex Cooper announces pregnancy with husband Matt Kaplan - AOL.com
fullText: The couple has been married since April 2024

text: ICC denies new arrest warrants for Israeli officials - The Jerusalem Post
fullText: The ICC said no new arrest warrants were issued in the “situation in the state of Palestine,” rejecting a Haaretz report on Israeli officials.

text: Canadian from hantavirus-hit cruise ship tests positive - BBC
fullText: The individual is one of four former passengers on the MV Hondius isolating on Vancouver Island, British Columbia.

text: For SpaceX, the stakes of next week’s Starship rocket test flight are sky-high - NBC News
fullText: SpaceX plans to launch a new version of its Starship rocket — a prototype of the system that NASA hopes will carry its astronauts to the moon — on Tuesday.

text: Adam Driver Brushes Off Lena Dunham Memoir Question in Cannes: ‘I’m Saving It All for My Book’ - Variety
fullText: Adam Driver brushed off a question on Lena Dunham's claims about his behavior on 'Girls' in her new memoir, saying: "I'm saving it all for my book."

text: Hundreds of millions of dollars for Trump’s ballroom ruled out of order in Senate - The Washington Post
fullText: The Senate parliamentarian determined that a proposal to fund $1 billion for White House security cannot be included as written.

text: Drone strike sparks a fire on the perimeter of UAE's nuclear power plant, shaking Iran war ceasefire - AP News
fullText: A drone strike has targeted the United Arab Emirates’ sole nuclear power plant, sparking a fire on its perimeter. There were no reports of injuries or radiological release. But Sunday's incident highlighted the risk of renewed war as the Iran ceasefire remain…

text: Deadly drone strikes on Moscow ‘entirely justified’, says Volodymyr Zelenskyy - Financial Times
fullText: Ukrainian president claims responsibility for attacks in retaliation for Russia’s bombardment of Kyiv

text: NFL: Aaron Rodgers agrees new one-year deal with Pittsburgh Steelers - reports - BBC
fullText: Quarterback Aaron Rodgers, 42, opts to continue his NFL career and agrees a new one-year deal with the Pittsburgh Steelers, according to reports.

text: Rival Airlines Are Carving Up Spirit’s Routes and Airport Slots - WSJ
A measles outbreak crossed into Mexico from Texas. A larger tragedy followed - CNN
fullText: It all started when a 9-year-old boy went with his parents to visit relatives in Seminole, Texas, early last year. After he returned home to Mexico, a red rash erupted on his skin. Within weeks, so many of his classmates fell ill that their school shut down.

text: Is it a problem if the Fed speaks too much? - CNN
fullText: The Federal Reserve is yapping too much about the economy, according to Kevin Warsh, the US central bank’s incoming leader.

text: A ‘Revival’ on the National Mall: White House Puts Prayer at Center of Washington - WSJ
Man ploughs car into crowd in Italy before trying to stab them - BBC
fullText: Eight people were injured, four seriously, before passers-by gave chase and stopped the man.

text: Iran war day 79: Tehran to unveil Hormuz toll plan; Israel bombs Lebanon - Al Jazeera
fullText: Iran says it will soon reveal the plan as Trump warns Tehran it will have a ‘very bad time’ if deal not reached soon.

text: ‘SNL’: Watch Paul McCartney Perform ‘Days We Left Behind,’ ‘Band on the Run’ - Rolling Stone
fullText: On SNL, Paul McCartney performs the lead single from his new album, his 1973 hit with Wings, and then makes a surprise encore.

text: WHO declares international emergency over Ebola outbreak - dw.com
fullText: The World Health Organization said the Ebola outbreak could be much larger than what is currently being detected and reported. It warned of health risks to other countries.
